questions.question:
Assertion (A): The common difference of the Arithmetic Progression $5, 2, -1, -4, \dots$ is $-3$. 
Reason (R): The common difference $d$ of an AP $a_1, a_2, a_3, \dots$ is given by $d = a_{n} - a_{n-1}$.

steps: Check the validity of Assertion (A): Calculate $d = a_2 - a_1 = 2 - 5 = -3$. The assertion is true.
Check the validity of Reason (R): The definition of common difference $d = a_n - a_{n-1}$ is the standard formula used in NCERT. The reason is true.
Determine if R explains A: Since the common difference formula is exactly what was used to verify the assertion, R is the correct explanation of A.
solution.derivation: d = a_2 - a_1 = 2 - 5 = -3 \implies \text{Assertion is true}. \text{Formula } d = a_n - a_{n-1} \text{ is correct definition} \implies \text{Reason is true}.

options: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
